perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each party and its Agents under t his Agreement shall [be continuing and shall survive the termination of any d iscussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the Proposal continue for a period of 5 (five) years.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of each party and its Agents under t his Agreement shall be continuing and shall survive the termination of any d iscussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the Proposal until either party provides 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the agreement. The original text states a fixed period of 5 years, while the modified text allows termination with 30 days' notice. This contradicts the fixed term elsewhere in the contract, specifically creating uncertainty about when the obligations of confidentiality actually end. It is unsure which term will be enforced due to the in-text contradiction regarding the agreement duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of each party and its Agents under t his Agreement shall be continuing and shall survive the termination of any d iscussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the Proposal until either party provides 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement duration from a fixed 5-year term to a term that can be terminated with 30 days written notice. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which uses the exact same phrasing. This creates an inconsistency regarding the length of the agreement, making it unclear which term applies, indicating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential and will not, without the Disclosing Par ty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally or in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential, and may, at its discretion, disclose Confidential Information to its parent company and legal counsel, regardless of Disclosing Party’s written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the Receiving Party's ability to disclose Confidential Information. While the original text prohibits disclosure without the Disclosing Party's written consent, the modified text allows disclosure to the Receiving Party's parent company and legal counsel without consent. This creates uncertainty about the restrictions on disclosing confidential information. This modifies the ability to disclose information creating an in-text contradiction regarding when information can be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will treat and keep all Confid ential Information as secret and confidential, and may, at its discretion, disclose Confidential Information to its parent company and legal counsel, regardless of Disclosing Party’s written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party cannot disclose Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's written consent. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information to its parent company and legal counsel regardless of the Disclosing Party's consent. This directly contradicts the original restriction, creating a clear in-text contradiction. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, this clearly shows the changed text contradicts itself, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation.
